diagrams.title: Contiguous Allocation Example
diagrams.description: Shows three files stored in consecutive disk blocks
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how contiguous allocation requires consecutive blocks and causes fragmentation when free blocks are not contiguous

diagrams.title: Linked Allocation with FAT
diagrams.description: Shows a file's blocks linked together using pointers stored in FAT
elements: FAT table entries for each block
Each FAT entry points to next block (-1 for EOF)
Free blocks maintained in separate free list
diagrams.purpose: Understand how linked allocation works without storing pointers in data blocks, using FAT as centralized pointer table

diagrams.title: Indexed Allocation Structure
diagrams.description: Shows file with index block pointing to all data blocks
elements: Index block (block 10) contains pointers to data blocks
All block addresses available in one place
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how indexed allocation enables fast random access by having all pointers in a single index block

diagrams.title: Unix Inode Structure
diagrams.description: Shows multilevel index structure with direct, single, double, and triple indirect pointers
elements: 1 single indirect pointer -> index block -> data blocks
1 double indirect pointer -> index of index blocks -> data blocks
1 triple indirect pointer -> multi-level index -> data blocks
diagrams.purpose: Understand how Unix inodes efficiently handle files of all sizes using increasing levels of indirection
